summary_paragraphs: Please find enclosed the complete forensic documentation package for the property at 6038 Pin Oak Ct, Royse City, TX 75189, USA. Our independent inspection has documented storm-related damage to the Roofing, Gutters.

The enclosed reports include annotated photography of all identified damage, an Xactimate-format replacement cost estimate, and a forensic causation analysis establishing the relationship between documented weather events and observed damage.
Please do not hesitate to contact us should you require any additional information or wish to schedule a joint inspection.

conclusion_paragraphs: Based on our forensic analysis of 50 documented findings, the property at 6038 Pin Oak Ct, Royse City, TX 75189, USA sustained confirmed storm damage from the March 05, 2024 hail and wind event. Chalk-tested hail impact squares on the front and right slopes document H=10+ hits per 10×10-foot area — exceeding the industry-recognized insurance replacement threshold of 8 hits per square — while circular impact depressions across at least four discrete locations confirm widespread, non-isolated damage distribution. Close-up inspection of individual impacts reveals granule displacement exposing the underlying asphalt mat, with soft-to-the-touch depressions confirming mat bruising and fracture consistent with functional hail damage per HAAG Engineering methodology. Additionally, wind damage is confirmed by broken seal strips and directional crease patterns near the nail line, establishing a combined peril loss affecting the entire roof field.

The confirmed hail and wind damage to the architectural laminated composite shingle roof system and associated components — including degraded ridge cap shingles exhibiting advanced granule erosion and compromised seal lines — requires full roof system replacement rather than spot repair. The density and uniform distribution of verified impacts across all inspected slopes precludes selective shingle replacement, as material matching of weathered architectural laminated shingles cannot be achieved to maintain manufacturer warranty coverage or waterproofing integrity per NRCA guidelines. Furthermore, replacement of the ridge cap is required because the existing deteriorated seal lines create a heightened susceptibility to wind uplift that cannot be remediated through localized treatment, and the TX-IRC mandates that roofing repairs restore the system to code-compliant condition.

Based on the documented damage, applicable building codes under the TX-IRC, and industry standards, our analysis identifies the need for full replacement of the main dwelling roof system including field shingles, ridge cap, underlayment, and associated flashings, as well as supplemental scope inclusion for the metal shop roof with spray foam insulation and the metal shed — structures confirmed as storm-damaged but not yet reflected in the carrier's current estimate. The carrier's current scope of $53,194.95 does not adequately address the documented conditions, as it omits the metal shop roof, metal shed, and contractor overhead and profit, which are warranted given the multi-trade coordination and scope complexity of this loss.

All findings and determinations in this report are grounded in HAAG Engineering standards for hail and wind damage identification, NRCA roofing installation and repair guidelines, and the International Residential Code as adopted in Texas (TX-IRC). The systematic chalk-circle test square methodology, soft-touch mat fracture confirmation, and multi-slope damage mapping collectively establish that the March 05, 2024 storm event caused pervasive functional damage to the roof system at 6038 Pin Oak Ct, Royse City, TX 75189, USA, warranting the full scope of replacement and supplemental items outlined herein.

executive_summary: On March 05, 2024, a combined hail and wind storm event impacted the property located at 6038 Pin Oak Ct, Royse City, TX 75189, USA, in Rockwall County, Texas. A comprehensive forensic inspection was conducted, producing 48 photographs documenting storm-related damage to the architectural laminated composite shingle roof system on the main dwelling, as well as a metal shop roof with spray foam insulation and an associated metal shed on the same property. This report establishes the causal relationship between the reported storm event and the observed damage across all roofing systems on the property.

Systematic chalk-circle testing across multiple roof slopes confirmed widespread hail impacts exhibiting circular depressions, granule displacement exposing the underlying asphalt mat, and confirmed mat bruising consistent with functional damage per HAAG Engineering standards. A documented test square on the front/right slope recorded ten or more hail strikes within a standard 10×10-foot area, exceeding accepted insurance replacement thresholds. Wind damage was simultaneously confirmed through broken seal strips, directional shingle lift patterns, and sharp wind-crease fold lines observed near nail lines on upper courses — a distribution pattern consistent with combined hail and wind exposure rather than isolated wear or aging.

Full replacement of the composite shingle roof system is technically required. The density and uniform distribution of confirmed hail impacts and wind damage across all inspected slopes preclude spot repair as a viable remediation strategy, as selective shingle replacement cannot restore the roof's compromised weather-tight integrity and will result in material-matching deficiencies in color, profile, and granule composition. Furthermore, any partial tear-off and re-roofing activity triggers compliance with the current Texas-adopted IRC (International Residential Code) provisions for underlayment, fastening schedules, and ventilation — code-upgrade requirements that apply to the full roof deck once repair scope exceeds localized patching thresholds.

The carrier's recognized scope at $53,194.95 does not appear to account for the metal shop roof with spray foam insulation, the metal shed structure, required overhead and profit for the multi-trade coordination involved, or the full extent of code-compliance upgrades triggered by roof system replacement under the Texas-adopted IRC. Our forensic analysis, grounded in 48 field photographs, 50 documented key findings, and standardized hail-testing methodology, identifies a total scope of necessary repairs that extends materially beyond the carrier's currently approved amount. A detailed line-item reconciliation accompanies this report.

photo_integrity.summary: All 19 photos show authentic roof inspection documentation consistent with standard hail damage assessment methodology. Chalk circles mark individual hail impacts on shingles, test square notations document hit counts per 10x10 area, and slope direction letters are present — all standard insurance inspection techniques. Granule loss patterns on shingles appear consistent with natural hail impact (rounded displacement, no sharp tool marks or artificial gouging). No digital manipulation artifacts, impossible shadows, cloned regions, or signs of staged damage were detected. Photos appear to be from a single consistent residential property in a suburban neighborhood. The inspection appears legitimate.
